timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $1.03B (+9.7% YoY) with Americas strength; net debt rose on revolver draw
FY2025 revenue $4.07B (+7.9%); operating income $859.5M; FCF $714.7M; dividend raised to $2.04/sh
Annual shareholder meeting; board reaffirmed capital return and electronic access strategy
FY2024 revenue $3.77B (+3.3%); net earnings $597.5M; continued share repurchases
Senior notes offering; extended debt maturity ladder for acquisition and return flexibility
FY2023 revenue $3.65B; operating margin recovery post supply-chain normalization
Debt refinancing via senior notes; fixed-rate ladder reduced near-term variable exposure
Spinoff from Ingersoll Rand as standalone Allegion plc; pure-play security products focus

industry.structure: Global security products market is fragmented but specification-grade mechanical and electronic access is concentrated among Allegion, Assa Abloy, dormakaba, and regional brands. Allegion leads North American commercial mechanical locks (Schlage, Von Duprin) with high installer and architect specification loyalty.
industry.share_shift: Electronic access and software are gaining share within new construction and retrofit; Allegion is migrating mix from pure mechanical toward connected products while defending Americas share against low-cost imports in commodity segments.
industry.trend: Non-residential construction activity, institutional retrofit, and electrification of access control drive demand; EMEA remains lower-margin and more price-competitive; variable-rate revolver usage adds financing sensitivity in 2025–2026.
